task_completion.description: Verify that the Fells Loop data is imported and visible in the mapping application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mapping application (e.g., Google Earth) is open and shows 'Fells Loop' in the places/layers panel or displayed on the map.

task_completion.description: Verify that the coordinate display format in the application is set to Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application's options or settings menu is open, and the 'Universal Transverse Mercator' (UTM) option is selected for coordinate display.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent views the properties or details of the waypoints to see their UTM coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A properties dialog or info window for a waypoint is open, displaying its coordinates in UTM format (Zone, Easting, Northing).

task_completion.description: Verify that the output text file is created or opened in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) is open, and the window title or save dialog shows the file name 'utm_coordinates.txt' located in 'C:\workspace\output'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the coordinates are recorded in the exact required format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains lines formatted exactly as 'WaypointName: ZoneDesignator Easting Northing' (e.g., 'Summit: 19T 327500 4703200') with no extra commas or characters.

task_completion.description: Verify that the recorded UTM coordinates fall within the expected real-world ranges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows the Zone as '19T', Easting values starting with 32... or 33... (between 320000 and 340000), and Northing values starting with 469... or 470... (between 4690000 and 4710000).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the mapping application's GUI to change the coordinate format.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the application's settings/options menu to select UTM, rather than using a terminal script to parse the GPX file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent extracted the data by interacting with the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent opening waypoint properties in the mapping software and typing/pasting them into the text editor, not running a Python/shell script to generate the text file automatically.

integrity.description: Verify the recorded coordinates match what is displayed in the mapping application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates typed into the text file should visually match the coordinates shown in the waypoint properties dialogs opened by the agent.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is exactly 'C:\workspace\output\utm_coordinates.txt'. The correct UTM zone for all Fells Loop waypoints is '19T'. Valid Easting values must fall between 320000 and 340000. Valid Northing values must fall between 4690000 and 4710000. The text format must be exactly 'WaypointName: ZoneDesignator Easting Northing' (e.g., 'Summit: 19T 327500 4703200').
